kids.name: Archetype 6: Vibrant Play
kids.rationale: Friendly, optimistic, and engaging. Designed to prevent intimidation for young students in underserved areas, using sky blue, sunny yellow, and rounded primary colors.

adults.name: Archetype 4: Swiss & High-Contrast
adults.rationale: Authoritative, clear, and function-first. Optimized for complex data consumption by teachers, parents, and government officials. Minimal distraction.

kids.card_style: rounded-3xl shadow-sm border-4 border-slate-100/50 hover:-translate-y-2 transition-transform duration-300
kids.alignment: Center-aligned headers, left-aligned body text. Generous breathing room.

adults.card_style: rounded-lg shadow-sm border border-slate-200 hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-200 bg-white
adults.alignment: Strict left-aligned grid alignment. Optical alignment for icons and text.

role_selection.layout: Split screen or central large bento grid. Visual distinction between 'I am a Student' (colorful, playful imagery) and adult roles (clean, icon-based).
role_selection.interaction: Large clickable areas with minimum min-h-16, hover scaling.

kids_dashboard.kpi_cards: Soft flat with thick pastel borders. Use lucide-react icons at large scale (e.g., size={40}) with colorful rounded backgrounds.
kids_dashboard.progress_bar: Thick (h-6), rounded-full, animated fill using --primary or --secondary colors.
kids_dashboard.ai_tutor_chat: Chat bubbles with rounded-2xl. AI messages in soft blue (bg-sky-50), user messages in soft yellow (bg-yellow-50). Action buttons below chat ('Explain Again', 'Make it Easier') as pill-shaped toggles.
kids_dashboard.badges_streaks: Use lucide-react 'Star', 'Flame', 'Award' heavily stylized with SVG filters, background glows, or wrapped in bright circular containers.

adults_dashboard.kpi_cards: Flat, rectangular, 1px border. Value in text-3xl font-semibold, label in text-sm uppercase tracking-wide.
adults_dashboard.charts: Use Recharts or Shadcn charts. Monochromatic blue scales for data, with semantic red/green only for negative/positive trends.
adults_dashboard.tables: Shadcn Table component. Dense layout, text-sm, sticky headers. Striped rows for readability.
adults_dashboard.teacher_workflow: Stepper component or vertical timeline for 'Record Today's Teaching'. Shadcn Select for class/subject choices.

kids.entrance: Bouncy staggered fade-ins using Framer Motion spring physics.
kids.feedback: Confetti bursts on task completion, bouncy loading dots.

adults.hover: Subtle background shifts (bg-slate-50 to bg-slate-100) or border color darkening.
adults.feedback: Crisp toast notifications (Sonner) in top-right corner.

hero_role_selection.description: Engaging, authentic photography of children in a learning environment to set the context.

teacher_avatar_placeholder.description: Professional, approachable teacher portrait for dashboard profiling.

instructions_to_main_agent.1: You MUST install and use 'lucide-react' for all icons. Avoid emojis.
instructions_to_main_agent.2: Create a unified React application with a router, but utilize layout wrappers to switch between the 'Kids Theme' (css variables, fonts, border radii) and 'Adults Theme'.
instructions_to_main_agent.3: Use Shadcn components for complex logic in both worlds, but highly override the Tailwind classes for the Kids dashboard to remove the 'Vercel look' (make them larger, rounder, more colorful).
instructions_to_main_agent.4: For all data visualizations in adult dashboards, use Recharts or Shadcn Chart components. Ensure color-blind-friendly palettes (use varied lightness, not just hue).
instructions_to_main_agent.5: Do NOT use any purple or teal-blue (#1B93A4) colors as instructed. Stick to the provided Sky Blue / Yellow (Kids) and Royal Blue / Slate (Adults) palettes.
instructions_to_main_agent.6: Add `data-testid` to EVERY interactive element (buttons, tabs, inputs, chat toggles) using kebab-case role-based naming (e.g., `data-testid='student-login-button'`).
instructions_to_main_agent.7: Do not use empty placeholder images. Use the Unsplash/Pexels URLs provided in the media_and_images section.
instructions_to_main_agent.8: Use `framer-motion` for kids' UI bouncy animations, but respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. Adult UI should rely on standard CSS transitions.
